name: Rupa Gosvami
dates_notes: Mughal court records (former finance minister); dates well-established.

dates_evidence.description: Mughal-period court records of his pre-conversion administrative role.
Manuscript colophons across the Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu and other works.

core_thesis:
Rūpa Gosvāmī's contribution is to convert Caitanya's experiential teaching into a systematic theology of *bhakti-rasa* that is simultaneously Sanskrit aesthetics and metaphysics. The conversion is precise rather than ornamental, and it does specific argumentative work for the *acintya-bhedābheda* programme.

The definition of *bhakti* is given at *Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu* 1.1.11: *anyābhilāṣitā-śūnyaṃ jñāna-karmādy-anāvṛtam ānukūlyena kṛṣṇānuśīlanaṃ bhaktir uttamā* — devotion uncontaminated by any other desire and unobstructed by the alternative *puruṣārthas* of *karma* and *jñāna*, the favourable cultivation of relation to Kṛṣṇa. Each qualifier marks a structural exclusion. *Anyābhilāṣitā-śūnyam* excludes desire for any object other than Kṛṣṇa. *Jñāna-karmādy-anāvṛtam* excludes the construal of *bhakti* as a means whose end lies in *jñāna* or *karma*; *bhakti* is its own *prayojana*.

The second move is the extension of classical *rasa*-theory. Bharata's *Nāṭya-Śāstra* and Abhinavagupta's *Abhinava-Bhāratī* establish a finite typology of aesthetic *rasas* with corresponding *sthāyi-bhāvas*. Rūpa argues that *bhakti* is itself a *rasa* — *bhakti-rasa* — with *prema* as its *sthāyi-bhāva* and Bhagavān as its *viṣaya-ālambana*. Five primary *rasas* are graded by intimacy: *śānta* (peaceful contemplation), *dāsya* (servant), *sakhya* (friend), *vātsalya* (parental), *mādhurya* (conjugal). The grading is not a hierarchy of esteem but a structural ordering of how the *jīva* relates to Kṛṣṇa under increasingly intimate *vibhāva*-conditions.

The ontological precondition is that the *jīva* is *taṭastha-śakti* of Bhagavān, eternally distinct and eternally non-different, constitutively constituted for relational *prema*. *Sādhana-bhakti* in its two modes — *vaidhī* (rule-governed) and *rāgānugā* (following spontaneous love of the eternal Vraja-residents) — matures through *bhāva* into *prema*. The goal is participation in Kṛṣṇa-*līlā* in one's *siddha-deha*, never absorption. Rūpa's framework is the doctrinal interface through which Caitanya's *acintya-bhedābheda* enters Sanskrit philosophical and aesthetic discourse and acquires the technical vocabulary by which it can be defended in those discourses' terms.

engaged_works.title: Bhakti-Rasamrta-Sindhu
engaged_works.summary: The *Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu* is the Gauḍīya school's foundational systematic treatise on *bhakti-rasa*. Four *vibhāgas* organise it: *pūrva* on the general nature of *bhakti*; *dakṣiṇa* on *sādhana-bhakti* in its *vaidhī* and *rāgānugā* modes; *paścima* on *bhāva-bhakti*; *uttara* on *prema-bhakti* and the analysis of the five primary *rasas*. The structural move that organises the whole is the transposition of the classical Sanskrit *rasa-sūtra* — Bharata's, Abhinavagupta's, Mammaṭa's — into a theological register. *Vibhāva*, *anubhāva*, *sāttvika-bhāva*, and *vyabhicāri-bhāva* combine in the qualified *bhakta* to generate the *sthāyi-bhāva* of devotion, by which Bhagavān is relished as *rasa* (*āsvāda*). The transposition is not metaphor: Rūpa treats the *rasa*-categories as functioning the same way they do in poetics, but with Bhagavān as the proper *ālambana*. The work serves three functions at once. It is a devotional manual, organising practice. It is a technical treatise within Sanskrit aesthetic theory, defensible to a *Naiyāyika* or an *Ālaṅkārika* on their own terms. And it is metaphysical exposition: the *jīva*'s status as eternal *taṭastha-śakti* is the precondition for *bhakti-rasa* being a real, eternal participation rather than a transient mood. The text becomes the principal Gauḍīya reference for all subsequent *rasa*-theological work and the structural basis for both Jīva's *Prīti-Sandarbha* and Viśvanātha Cakravartī's manuals.

engaged_works.title: Ujjvala-Nilamani
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored extension of Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu on madhura-rasa.
engaged_works.summary: The *Ujjvala-Nīlamaṇi* extends the *Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu*'s *uttara-vibhāga* into a dedicated treatise on *madhura-rasa* — the supreme erotic-devotional mode in which Kṛṣṇa is related to as *priyatama* and the paradigmatic *bhaktas* are the *gopīs* of Vraja, with Rādhā as their crown. Rūpa supplies a granular technical taxonomy: classification of *nāyikā*-types and *nāyaka*-types adapted from classical poetics, the ten stages of *prema* (from *prema* through *mahābhāva*), the conditions and *anubhāvas* of *vipralambha* (love-in-separation) and *sambhoga* (union), and the structural place of *adhirūḍha-mahābhāva* in Rādhā alone. The work's metaphysical load is significant: it treats *mādhurya-rasa* not as one option among several but as the most complete actualisation of *acintya-bhedābheda*, in which the *jīva*'s real distinctness and real non-difference from Bhagavān are simultaneously most acute. It is the canonical Gauḍīya reference for Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa rasa-theology.

engaged_works.title: Laghu-Bhagavatamrta
engaged_works.summary: The *Laghu-Bhāgavatāmṛta* is Rūpa's compact but doctrinally dense compendium of *bhāgavata-tattva*, organised into two parts: *Kṛṣṇa-amṛta* (the nature of Bhagavān, his *avatāras*, and the doctrine of Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān*) and *Bhakta-amṛta* (the typology of devotees and the structure of *bhakti*). The work constructs the canonical Gauḍīya hierarchy of divine manifestation — *svayaṃ-rūpa*, *tad-ekātma-rūpa*, and *āveśa-rūpa* — and defends, by anchored *Bhāgavata* citation, the school-defining thesis that Kṛṣṇa is not an *avatāra* of Viṣṇu but *svayaṃ-bhagavān*, the source of all other manifestations including Viṣṇu. It supplies the typology of *bhakti*-stages and devotee-classes that Jīva will then expand in the *Bhagavat*-, *Kṛṣṇa*-, and *Bhakti-Sandarbhas*. Concise enough to be memorised and exegetically dense enough to license sub-commentary, the *Laghu-Bhāgavatāmṛta* functions as the school's portable doctrinal map of Bhagavān and his *bhaktas*.
